key_insight: Unsupervised pre-annotation can dramatically speed up pixel-level semantic segmentation labeling in industrial materials science, cutting annotation time by about 78%.
engineering_relevance: This makes high-resolution microstructure dataset creation more practical for deploying deep learning in industrial inspection and steel characterization workflows.
method: Applied unsupervised computer vision algorithms as a pre-annotation step for semantic segmentation.
Compared labeling time from scratch versus assisted annotation on large, high-resolution microstructure images.
Built and released a public steel microstructure segmentation dataset and trained a validated deep learning model.
evidence_signal: On the reported dataset, annotation time dropped from 170 hours to 37 hours, with images as large as 1280×959 and 960×703.
watch_item: Time savings depend on image complexity and the quality of unsupervised pre-annotations, so gains may vary across materials and segmentation tasks.
paper_title: Speeding up the annotation process in semantic segmentation industrial applications
